title: Operators can steer running procedures from procedure chat
description:
Feature: Live procedure steering

Scenario: Steering message is sent from a running procedure conversation
Given an operator is viewing a procedure conversation
When they type a chat message into the procedure conversation input
Then the message is persisted as a completed user steering note
And it does not dispatch a console chat response

Scenario: Steering influences future agent calls
Given a procedure has received a steering note
When any procedure agent makes a future LLM call
Then the steering note is included as advisory context before the call
And each agent receives the steering note at most once

Scenario: Steering reaches summaries and reports
Given a steering note was sent before a summary or report agent call
When that agent builds its LLM context
Then the steering note is included so summaries, diagnostics, and prescriptions can be influenced.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Implementation started on feature/plx-402e58-execute-tactus-integration. Initial findings: existing dashboard input creates pending console-chat messages; optimizer has a mailbox block but it consumes the wrong message shape and should be replaced by one central steering path.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Implemented the live procedure steering behavior: dashboard messages persist as completed steering notes without console dispatch, and runtime agents retrieve steering context for future LLM calls. Focused dashboard and Python tests pass.
